title: CloudFront Cache Keys, Origin Requests, TTLs, and Invalidation · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs cloudfront cache keys, origin requests, ttls, and invalidation from browser, Go service, S3, CloudFront, security, billing, and stakeholder evidence, then challenges one retained misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for cache-key, forwarding, and freshness contract after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Include only representation-changing path, query, header, cookie, encoding, and authorization dimensions in the cache key.
Use origin request policy for additional required viewer data without unnecessary cache fragmentation or trust escalation.
Combine origin Cache-Control, minimum, default, maximum, stale behavior, validators, and error caching with content-change and privacy requirements.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Cache Key Identity · changed case · Review Cache Key Identity
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Go file-delivery team handles case 6560 during a cost-anomaly investigation. Build deterministic Go evidence to align cache identity, origin forwarding, TTL, versioned names, and urgent invalidation; preserve accessible progress, cancellation, status, and recovery evidence; then change cache and retrieval cost assumptions and defend the new budget result. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Include only representation-changing path, query, header, cookie, encoding, and authorization dimensions in the cache key.

Which action demonstrates Cache Key Identity without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Cache Key Identity · changed case · Review Cache Key Identity.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Configure Brotli or gzip variants, Accept-Encoding cache behavior, already-compressed media, content length, and changed client capability evidence.

hints: For S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Compression Vary, name the stakeholder, file, object version, authorization, byte, time, concurrency, cache, cost, cleanup, and recovery boundary, Compression Vary precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Compression Vary, trace this concrete input and predict authorization, custody, integrity, range, cache, retry, cost, cleanup, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A review Go file-delivery team handles case 6369 during an SDK and infrastructure canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to align cache identity, origin forwarding, TTL, versioned names, and urgent invalidation; record cleanup, rollback, restoration, and residual-risk ownership; then cancel halfway through and prove every body, part, and goroutine reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Configure Brotli or gzip variants, Accept-Encoding cache behavior, already-compressed media, content length, and changed client capability evidence.

For S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Compression Vary, challenge the documented misconception with file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: CloudFront compression reduces every file type and never changes cache identity.
